1.1: In the beginning was the Word {Logos}, and the Word was with God {Theon}, and the Word was God {Theos}.
1.2: He was with God {Theon} in the beginning.
1.3: Through Him all things were made, and without Him nothing was made that has been made.
1.4: In Him was life, and that life was the light of men.
1.5: The Light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome (comprehended) it.
1.6: There came a man who was sent from God {Theou}. His name was John (YHWH is gracious).
1.7: He came as a witness to testify about the Light, so that through him everyone might believe.
1.8: He himself was not the Light, but he came to testify about the Light.
1.9: The true Light, who gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
1.10: He was in the world, and though the world was made through Him, the world did not recognize Him.
1.11: He came to His own, and His own did not receive Him.
1.12: But to all who did receive Him, to those who believed in His name, He gave the right to become children of God {Theou}—
1.13: children born not of blood, nor of the desire or will of man, but born of God {Theou}.
1.14: The Word {Logos} became flesh and made His dwelling among us (and tabernacled among us). We have seen His glory {doxan}, the glory of the one and only Son (the Only Begotten) from the Father, full of grace and truth.
1.15: John (YHWH is gracious) testified concerning Him. He cried out, saying, “This is He of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me because He was before me.’
1.16: From His fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
1.17: For the law was given through Moses (drawn out); grace and truth came through Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One).
1.18: No one has ever seen God {Theon}, but the one and only Son, who is Himself God {Theos} and [but the only begotten Son, who] is at the Father’s side \in the Father’s bosom/, has made Him known.
1.19: And this was John’s (YHWH is gracious) testimony when the Jews (people of praise) of Jerusalem (city of peace) sent priests and Levites to ask him, “Who are you?
1.20: He did not refuse to confess, but openly declared, “I am not the Christ (the Anointed One).
1.21: “Then who are you? they inquired. “Are you Elijah (my God is YHWH)? He said, “I am not. “Are you the Prophet? He answered, “No.
1.22: So they said to him, “Who are you? We need an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?
1.23: John replied in the words of Isaiah (YHWH saves) the prophet: “I am a voice of one calling in the wilderness (solitary place), ‘Make straight the way for the Lord .’”
1.24: Then the Pharisees who had been sent
1.25: asked him, “Why then do you baptize (submerge), if you are not the Christ (the Anointed One), nor Elijah (my God is YHWH), nor the Prophet?
1.26: “I baptize (submerge) with (in) water, John (YHWH is gracious) replied, “but among you? stands One you? do not know.
1.27: He is the One who comes after me, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.
1.28: All this happened at Bethany (house of affliction) beyond the Jordan (flowing down), where John (YHWH is gracious) was baptizing (submerging).
1.29: The next day John saw Jesus (YHWH saves) coming toward him and said, “Look (behold), the Lamb of God {Theou}, who takes away the sin of the world!
1.30: This is He of whom I said, ‘A man who comes after me has surpassed me because He was before me.
1.31: I myself did not know Him, but the reason I came baptizing (submerging) with water was that He might be revealed to Israel (He wrestles with God).
1.32: Then John (YHWH is gracious) testified, “I saw the Spirit {Pneuma} descending from heaven like a dove and resting on Him.
1.33: I myself did not know Him, but the One who sent me to baptize (submerge) with water told me, ‘The man on whom you see the Spirit {Pneuma} descend and rest is He who will baptize with the Holy Spirit {Hagi? Pneumati}.
1.34: I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God [the Chosen One of God].
1.35: The next day John (YHWH is gracious) was there again with two of his disciples (pupils).
1.36: When he saw Jesus (YHWH saves) walking by, he said, “Look (behold), the Lamb of God {Theou}!
1.37: And when the two disciples (pupils) heard him say this, they followed Jesus (YHWH saves).
1.38: Jesus (YHWH saves) turned and saw them following. “What do you? want? He asked. They said to Him, “Rabbi (which means Teacher {Didaskale}), “where are You staying?
1.39: “Come and see, He replied. So they went and saw where He was staying, and spent that day with Him. It was about the tenth hour (about 4 pm).
1.40: Andrew (manly), Simon (hearing) Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard John’s testimony and followed (accompanied) Jesus.
1.41: He first found his brother Simon (hearing) and told him, “We have found the Messiah (the Anointed One)” (which is translated as Christ).
1.42: Andrew brought him to Jesus (YHWH saves), who looked at him and said, “You are Simon (hearing) son of John (YHWH is gracious). You will be called Cephas (Peter) (which is translated as Peter).
1.43: The next day Jesus decided to set out for Galilee (circuit). Finding Philip (friend of horses), He told him, “Follow (accompany) Me.
1.44: Now Philip (friend of horses) was from Bethsaida (fishing house), the same town as Andrew (manly) and Peter (rock).
1.45: Philip (friend of horses) found Nathanael (God has given) and told him, “We have found the One Moses (drawn out) wrote about in the Law, the One the prophets foretold—Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch), the son of Joseph (he will add).
1.46: “Can anything good come from Nazareth (branch)? Nathanael (God has given) asked. “Come and see (behold), said Philip (friend of horses).
1.47: When Jesus (YHWH saves) saw Nathanael (God has given) approaching, He said of him, “Here (behold) is a true Israelite, in whom there is no deceit.
1.48: “How do You know me? Nathanael (God has given) asked. Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Before Philip (friend of horses) called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
1.49: “Rabbi, Nathanael (God has given) answered, “You are the Son of God {Theou}! You are the King of Israel (He wrestles with God)!
1.50: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to him, “Do you believe just because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these.
1.51: Then He declared, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, you? will all see heaven open and the angels (messengers) of God {Theou} ascending and descending on the Son of Man .”

2.1: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee (circuit). Jesus’ mother was there,
2.2: and Jesus (YHWH saves) and His disciples (pupils) had also been invited to the wedding.
2.3: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to Him, “They have no more wine.
2.4: “Woman, what is that to you and to Me? Jesus (YHWH saves) replied. “My hour has not yet come.
2.5: His mother said to the servants {diakonois}, “Do whatever He tells you?.
2.6: Now six stone water jars had been set there for the Jewish rites of purification. Each could hold from twenty to thirty gallons <20.8 to 31.2 gal / 78.8 to 118.1 l>.
2.7: Jesus (YHWH saves) told the servants, “Fill the jars with water. So they filled them to the brim.
2.8: “Now draw some out, He said, “and take it to the master of the banquet. They did so,
2.9: and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine. He did not know where it was from, but the servants {diakonoi} who had drawn the water knew. Then he called the bridegroom aside
2.10: and said, “Everyone serves the fine wine first, and then the cheap wine after the guests are drunk. But you have saved the fine wine until now!
2.11: Jesus (YHWH saves) performed this, the first of His signs, at Cana in Galilee (circuit). He thus revealed (made clear) His glory {doxan}, and His disciples (pupils) believed (had faith) in Him.
2.12: After this, He went down to Capernaum (village of comfort) with His mother and brothers and His disciples (pupils), and they stayed there a few days.
2.13: When the Jewish Passover was near, Jesus (YHWH saves) went up to Jerusalem (city of peace).
2.14: In the temple courts \the temple/ He found men selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and money changers seated at their tables.
2.15: So He made a whip out of cords and drove all from the temple courts, both sheep and cattle. He poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
2.16: To those selling doves He said, “Get these out of here! How dare you turn My Father’s house into a marketplace!
2.17: His disciples (pupils) remembered that it is written: “Zeal for Your house will consume Me .”
2.18: On account of this, the Jews (people of praise) demanded, “What sign can You show us to prove Your authority to do these things?
2.19: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up again.
2.20: “This temple took forty-six years to build, the Jews (people of praise) replied, “and You are going to raise it up in three days?
2.21: But Jesus was speaking about the temple of His body.
2.22: After He was raised from the dead, His disciples (pupils) remembered that He had said this. Then they believed (had faith) the Scripture and the word that Jesus (YHWH saves) had spoken.
2.23: While He was in Jerusalem (city of peace) at the Passover Feast, many people saw the signs He was doing and believed (had faith) in His name.
2.24: But Jesus (YHWH saves) did not entrust Himself to them, for He knew them all.
2.25: He did not need any testimony about man, for He knew what was in a man.

3.1: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus (victory of the people), a leader of the Jews (people of praise).
3.2: He came to Jesus at night and said, “Rabbi, we know that You are a teacher {didaskalos} who has come from God {Theou}. For no one could perform the signs You are doing if God {Theos} were not with him.
3.3: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God {Theou} unless he is born again (born from above).
3.4: “How can a man be born when he is old? Nicodemus (victory of the people) asked. “Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time to be born?
3.5: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “Truly, truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God {Theou} unless he is born of water and the Spirit {Pneumatos}.
3.6: Flesh is born of flesh, but spirit {pneuma} is born of the Spirit {Pneumatos}.
3.7: Do not be amazed that I said, ‘You? must be born again.
3.8: The wind {pneuma} blows where it wishes. You hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit {Pneumatos}.
3.9: “How can this be? Nicodemus (victory of the people) asked.
3.10: “You are Israel’s teacher {didaskalos}, said Jesus (YHWH saves), “and you do not understand these things?
3.11: Truly, truly, I tell you, we speak of what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, and yet you? people do not accept our testimony.
3.12: If I have told you? about earthly things and you? do not believe, how will you? believe if I tell you? about heavenly things?
3.13: No one has ascended into heaven except the One who descended from heaven—the Son of Man [who is in heaven.].
3.14: Just as Moses (drawn out) lifted up the snake in the wilderness (solitary place), so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
3.15: that everyone who believes in Him may have eternal life.
3.16: For God {Theos} so loved {?gap?sen} the world that He gave His one and only (only begotten) Son, that everyone who believes in Him shall not perish but have eternal life.
3.17: For God {Theos} did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through Him.
3.18: Whoever believes in Him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe has already been condemned, because he has not believed in the name of God’s {Theou} one and only Son.
3.19: And this is the verdict: The Light has come into the world, but men loved {?gap?san} the darkness rather than the Light because their deeds (acts) were evil.
3.20: Everyone who does evil hates the Light, and does not come into the Light for fear that his deeds (acts) will be exposed.
3.21: But whoever practices the truth comes into the Light, so that it may be seen clearly that what he has done has been accomplished in God {The?}.”
3.22: After this, Jesus (YHWH saves) and His disciples (pupils) went into the Judean countryside, where He spent some time with them and baptized (submerged).
3.23: Now John (YHWH is gracious) was also baptizing (submerging) at Aenon near Salim, because the water was plentiful there, and people kept coming to be baptized (submerged).
3.24: (For John (YHWH is gracious) had not yet been thrown into prison.)
3.25: Then a dispute arose between John’s (YHWH is gracious) disciples (pupils) and a certain Jew [and the Jews] over the issue of ceremonial washing.
3.26: So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Look (behold), Rabbi, the One who was with you beyond the Jordan (flowing down), the One you testified about—He is baptizing (submerging), and everyone is going to Him.
3.27: John (YHWH is gracious) replied, “A man can receive only what is given him from heaven.
3.28: You? yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ (the Anointed One), but am sent ahead of Him.
3.29: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom stands and listens for him, and is overjoyed to hear the bridegroom’s voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.
3.30: He must increase; I must decrease.
3.31: The One who comes from above is above all. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The One who comes from heaven is above all [The One comes from heaven.].
3.32: He testifies to what He has seen and heard, yet no one accepts His testimony.
3.33: Whoever accepts His testimony has certified that God {Theos} is truthful.
3.34: For the One whom God {Theos} has sent speaks the words {rh?mata} of God {Theou}, for God gives the Spirit {Pneuma} without limit.
3.35: The Father {Pat?r} loves {agapa} the Son and has placed all things in His hands.
3.36: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever rejects the Son will not see life. Instead, the wrath of God {Theou} remains on him.”

4.1: When Jesus (YHWH saves) realized that the Pharisees were aware He \When therefore Jesus knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus/ was gaining and baptizing (submerging) more disciples (pupils) than John (YHWH is gracious)
4.2: (although it was not Jesus (YHWH saves) who baptized (submerged), but His disciples (pupils)),
4.3: He left Judea (land of praise) and returned to Galilee (circuit).
4.4: Now He had to pass through Samaria.
4.5: So He came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob (he grasps the heel) had given to his son Joseph (he will add).
4.6: Since Jacob’s well was there, Jesus (YHWH saves), weary from His journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour (about noon).
4.7: When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus (YHWH saves) said to her, “Give Me a drink.
4.8: (His disciples (pupils) had gone into the town to buy food.)
4.9: “You are a Jew, said the woman. “How can You ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman? (For Jews (people of praise) do not associate with Samaritans.)
4.10: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “If you knew the gift of God {Theou} and who is asking you for a drink, you would have asked Him, and He would have given you living water.
4.11: “Sir {Kyrie}, the woman replied, “You have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where then will You get this living water?
4.12: Are You greater than our father Jacob (he grasps the heel), who gave us this well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock?
4.13: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to her, “Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
4.14: But whoever drinks the water I give him will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give him will become in him a fount of water springing up to eternal life.
4.15: The woman said to Him, “Sir {Kyrie}, give me this water so that I will not get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water.
4.16: Jesus told her, “Go, call your husband and come back.
4.17: “I have no husband, the woman replied. Jesus (YHWH saves) said to her, “You are correct to say that you have no husband.
4.18: In fact, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband. You have spoken truthfully.
4.19: “Sir {Kyrie}, the woman said, “I see that You are a prophet.
4.20: Our fathers {pateres} worshiped on this mountain, but you? Jews say that the place where one must worship is in Jerusalem (city of peace).
4.21: “Believe Me, woman, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “a time is coming when you? will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem (city of peace).
4.22: You? worship what you? do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews (people of praise).
4.23: But a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit {pneumati} and in truth, for the Father {Pat?r} is seeking such as these to worship Him.
4.24: God is Spirit {Pneuma}, and His worshipers must worship Him in spirit {pneumati} and in truth.
4.25: The woman said, “I know that Messiah (the Anointed One)” (called Christ) “is coming. When He comes, He will explain everything to us.
4.26: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “I who speak to you am He.
4.27: Just then His disciples (pupils) returned and were surprised that He was speaking with a woman. But no one asked Him, “What do You want from her? or “Why are You talking with her?
4.28: Then the woman left her water jar, went back into the town, and said to the people,
4.29: “Come, see (behold) a man who told me everything I ever did. Could this be the Christ (the Anointed One)?
4.30: So they left the town and made their way toward Jesus.
4.31: Meanwhile the disciples (pupils) urged Him, “Rabbi, eat something.
4.32: But He told them, “I have food to eat that you? know nothing about.
4.33: So the disciples (pupils) asked one another, “Could someone have brought Him food?
4.34: Jesus (YHWH saves) explained, “My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to finish His work.
4.35: Do you? not say, ‘There are still four months until the harvest’? I tell you?, lift up your? eyes and look at the fields, for they are ripe \white/ for harvest.
4.36: Already the reaper draws his wages and gathers a crop for eternal life, so that the sower and the reaper may rejoice together.
4.37: For in this case the saying ‘One sows and another reaps is true.
4.38: I sent you? to reap what you? have not worked for; others have done the hard work, and now you? have taken up their labor.
4.39: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed (had faith) in Jesus because of the woman’s testimony, “He told me everything I ever did.
4.40: So when the Samaritans came to Him, they asked Him to stay with them, and He stayed two days.
4.41: And many more believed (had faith) because of His message.
4.42: They said to the woman, “We now believe not only because of your words; we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man truly is the Savior {S?t?r} of the world.
4.43: After two days, Jesus left for Galilee (circuit).
4.44: Now He Himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in his own hometown.
4.45: Yet when He arrived, the Galileans welcomed Him. They had seen all the great things He had done in Jerusalem (city of peace) at the feast, for they had gone there as well.
4.46: So once again He came to Cana in Galilee (circuit), where He had turned the water into wine. And there was a royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum (village of comfort).
4.47: When he heard that Jesus (YHWH saves) had come from Judea (land of praise) to Galilee (circuit), he went and begged Him to come down and heal his son, who was about to die.
4.48: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to him, “Unless you? people see signs and wonders, you? will never believe.
4.49: “Sir {Kyrie}, the official said, “come down before my child dies.
4.50: “Go, said Jesus (YHWH saves). “Your son will live. The man took Jesus at His word and departed.
4.51: And while he was still on the way, his servants {douloi} met him with the news that his boy was alive.
4.52: So he inquired as to the hour when his son had recovered, and they told him, “The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour (one in the afternoon).
4.53: Then the father {pat?r} realized that this was the very hour in which Jesus (YHWH saves) had told him, “Your son will live. And he and all his household believed (had faith).
4.54: This was now the second sign that Jesus (YHWH saves) performed after coming from Judea (land of praise) into Galilee (circuit).

5.1: Some time later there was a feast of the Jews (people of praise), and Jesus (YHWH saves) went up to Jerusalem (city of peace).
5.2: Now there is in Jerusalem (city of peace) near the Sheep Gate a pool with five covered colonnades, which in Hebrew (in Aramaic) is called Bethesda [Bethzatha].
5.3: On these walkways \In these/ lay a great number of the sick, the blind, the lame, and the paralyzed [awaiting the moving of the waters. 4 For from time to time an angel descended into the pool and stirred the water. As soon as it was stirred, the first to enter the pool would be healed of his disease].
5.5: One man there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
5.6: When Jesus (YHWH saves) saw him lying there and realized that he had spent a long time in this condition, He asked him, “Do you want to get well?
5.7: “Sir {Kyrie}, the invalid replied, “I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am on my way, someone else goes in before me.
5.8: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) told him, “Get up, pick up your mat, and walk.
5.9: Immediately the man was made well, and he picked up his mat and began to walk. Now this happened on the Sabbath day,
5.10: so the Jews (people of praise) said to the man who had been healed, “This is the Sabbath! It is unlawful for you to carry your mat.
5.11: But he answered, “The man who made me well told me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.
5.12: “Who is this man who told you to pick it up and walk? they asked.
5.13: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus (YHWH saves) had slipped away while the crowd was there.
5.14: Afterward, Jesus (YHWH saves) found the man at the temple and said to him, “See (behold), you have been made well. Stop sinning, or something worse may happen to you.
5.15: And the man went away and told the Jews (people of praise) that it was Jesus (YHWH saves) who had made him well.
5.16: Now because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath, the Jews (people of praise) began to persecute Him.
5.17: But Jesus (YHWH saves) answered them, “To this very day My Father {Pat?r} is at His work, and I too am working.
5.18: Because of this, the Jews (people of praise) tried all the harder to kill Him. Not only was He breaking the Sabbath, but He was even calling God {Theon} His own Father {Patera}, making Himself equal with God {The?}.
5.19: So Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, the Son can do nothing by Himself, unless He sees the Father {Patera} doing it. For whatever the Father does, the Son also does.
5.20: The Father {Pat?r} loves the Son and shows Him all He does. And to your? amazement, He will show Him even greater works (acts) than these.
5.21: For just as the Father {Pat?r} raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whom He wishes.
5.22: Furthermore, the Father {Pat?r} judges no one, but has assigned all judgment to the Son,
5.23: so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father {Patera}. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.
5.24: Truly, truly, I tell you?, whoever hears My word and believes Him who sent Me has eternal life and will not come under judgment. Indeed, he has crossed over from death to life.
5.25: Truly, truly, I tell you?, the hour is coming and has now come when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God {Theou}, and those who hear will live.
5.26: For as the Father {Pat?r} has life in Himself, so also He has granted the Son to have life in Himself.
5.27: And He has given Him authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of Man.
5.28: Do not be amazed at this, for the hour is coming when all who are in their graves will hear His voice
5.29: and come out—those who have done good to the resurrection (rising again) of life, and those who have done evil to the resurrection of judgment .
5.30: I can do nothing by Myself; I judge only as I hear. And My judgment is just, because I do not seek My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
5.31: If I testify about Myself, My testimony is not valid.
5.32: There is another who testifies about Me, and I know that His testimony about Me is valid.
5.33: You? have sent to John (YHWH is gracious), and he has testified to the truth.
5.34: Even though I do not accept human testimony, I say these things so that you? may be saved.
5.35: John was a lamp that burned and gave light, and you? were willing for a season to bask in his light.
5.36: But I have testimony more substantial than that of John (YHWH is gracious). For the works (acts) that the Father {Pat?r} has given Me to accomplish—the very works I am doing—testify about Me that the Father has sent Me.
5.37: And the Father {Pat?r} who sent Me has Himself testified about Me. You? have never heard His voice nor seen His form,
5.38: nor does His word abide in you?, because you? do not believe the One He sent.
5.39: You? pore over the Scriptures (Writings) because you? presume that by them you possess eternal life. These are the very words that testify about Me,
5.40: yet you? refuse to come to Me to have life.
5.41: I do not accept glory {Doxan} from men,
5.42: but I know you?, that you? do not have the love {agap?n} of God {Theou} within you.
5.43: I have come in My Father’s name, and you? have not received Me; but if someone else comes in his own name, you? will receive him.
5.44: How can you? believe if you? accept glory {doxan} from one another, yet do not seek the glory that comes from the only God {Theou}?
5.45: Do not think that I will accuse you? before the Father {Patera}. Your? accuser is Moses (drawn out), in whom you? have put your? hope.
5.46: If you? had believed (had faith) Moses (drawn out), you? would believe (have faith) Me, because he wrote about Me.
5.47: But since you? do not believe what he wrote, how will you? believe what I say?

6.1: After this, Jesus (YHWH saves) crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (circuit) (that is, the Sea of Tiberias).
6.2: A large crowd followed Him because they saw the signs He was performing on the sick.
6.3: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) went up on the mountain and sat down with His disciples (pupils).
6.4: Now the Jewish Feast of the Passover was near.
6.5: When Jesus (YHWH saves) looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward Him, He said to Philip (friend of horses), “Where can we buy bread for these people to eat?
6.6: But He was asking this to test him, for He knew what He was about to do.
6.7: Philip (friend of horses) answered, “Two hundred denarii (days’ wages) would not buy enough bread for each of them to have a small piece.
6.8: One of His disciples (pupils), Andrew (manly), Simon (hearing) Peter’s brother, said to Him,
6.9: “Here is a boy with five barley loaves and two small fish. But what difference will these make among so many?
6.10: “Have the people sit down, Jesus (YHWH saves) said. Now there was plenty of grass in that place, so the men sat down, about five thousand of them.
6.11: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) took the loaves and the fish, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted.
6.12: And when everyone was full, He said to His disciples (pupils), “Gather the pieces that are left over, so that nothing will be wasted.
6.13: So they collected them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.
6.14: When the people saw the sign that Jesus had performed [He had performed], they began to say, “Truly this is the Prophet who is to come into the world.
6.15: Then Jesus (YHWH saves), realizing that they were about to come and make Him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by Himself.
6.16: When evening came, His disciples (pupils) went down to the sea,
6.17: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum (village of comfort). It was already dark, and Jesus (YHWH saves) had not yet gone out to them.
6.18: A strong wind was blowing, and the sea grew agitated.
6.19: When they had rowed about three or four miles <2.87 to 3.45 mi / 4.62 to 5.55 km>, they saw Jesus (YHWH saves) approaching the boat, walking on the sea—and they were terrified.
6.20: But Jesus spoke up: “It is I; do not be afraid.
6.21: Then they were willing to take Him into the boat, and at once the boat reached the shore where they were heading.
6.22: The next day, the crowd that had remained on the other side of the sea realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus (YHWH saves) had not boarded it with His disciples (pupils), but they had gone away alone.
6.23: However, some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord {Kyriou} had given thanks.
6.24: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus (YHWH saves) nor His disciples (pupils) were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum (village of comfort) to look for Him.
6.25: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they asked Him, “Rabbi, when did You get here?
6.26: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, it is not because you? saw these signs that you? are looking for Me, but because you? ate the loaves and had your? fill.
6.27: Do not work for food that perishes, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you?. For on Him God {Theos} the Father {Pat?r} has placed His seal of approval.
6.28: Then they inquired, “What must we do to perform the works (acts) of God {Theou}?
6.29: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “The work of God {Theou} is this: to believe in the One He has sent.
6.30: So they asked Him, “What sign then will You perform, so that we may see it and believe You? What will You do?
6.31: Our fathers {pateres} ate the manna in the wilderness (solitary place), as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat .’
6.32: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, it was not Moses (drawn out) who gave you? the bread from heaven, but it is My Father {Pat?r} who gives you? the true bread from heaven.
6.33: For the bread of God {Theou} is He who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
6.34: “Sir {Kyrie}, they said, “give us this bread at all times.
6.35: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to Me will never hunger, and whoever believes in Me will never thirst.
6.36: But as I stated, you? have seen Me and still you do not believe.
6.37: Everyone the Father {Pat?r} gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will never drive away.
6.38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but to do the will of Him who sent Me.
6.39: And this is the will of Him who sent Me, that I shall lose none of those He has given Me, but raise them up at the last day.
6.40: For it is My Father’s will that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in Him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
6.41: At this, the Jews (people of praise) began to grumble about Jesus because He had said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.
6.42: They were asking, “Is this not Jesus (YHWH saves), the son of Joseph (he will add), whose father {patera} and mother we know? How then can He say, ‘I have come down from heaven
6.43: “Stop grumbling among yourselves, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied.
6.44: “No one can come to Me unless the Father {Pat?r} who sent Me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.
6.45: It is written in the Prophets: ‘And they will all be taught {didaktoi} by God .’ Everyone who has heard the Father and learned from Him comes to Me—
6.46: not that anyone has seen the Father {Patera} except the One who is from God {Theou}; only He has seen the Father.
6.47: Truly, truly, I tell you?, he who believes has eternal life.
6.48: I am the bread of life.
6.49: Your? fathers {pateres} ate the manna in the wilderness (solitary place), yet they died.
6.50: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that anyone may eat of it and not die.
6.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever \to the age/. And this bread, which I will give for the life of the world, is My flesh.
6.52: At this, the Jews (people of praise) began to argue among themselves, “How can this man give us His flesh to eat?
6.53: So Jesus (YHWH saves) said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, unless you? eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man, you? have no life in you.
6.54: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
6.55: For My flesh is real food, and My blood is real drink.
6.56: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood remains in Me, and I in him.
6.57: Just as the living Father {Pat?r} sent Me and I live because of the Father {Patera}, so also the one who feeds on Me will live because of Me.
6.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your fathers {pateres}, who ate the manna and died, the one who eats this bread will live forever \to the age/.
6.59: Jesus said this while teaching {didask?n} in the synagogue in Capernaum (village of comfort).
6.60: On hearing it, many of His disciples (pupils) said, “This is a difficult teaching. Who can accept it?
6.61: Aware that His disciples (pupils) were grumbling about this teaching, Jesus (YHWH saves) asked them, “Does this offend you??
6.62: Then what will happen if you? see the Son of Man ascend to where He was before?
6.63: The Spirit {pneuma} gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words {rh?mata} I have spoken to you? are spirit and they are life.
6.64: However, there are some of you? who do not believe. (For Jesus (YHWH saves) had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray Him.)
6.65: Then Jesus said, “This is why I told you? that no one can come to Me unless the Father has granted it to him.
6.66: From that time on many of His disciples (pupils) turned back and no longer walked with Him.
6.67: So Jesus (YHWH saves) asked the Twelve, “Do you? want to leave too?
6.68: Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) replied, “Lord {Kyrie}, to whom would we go? You have the words {rh?mata} of eternal life.
6.69: We believe and know that You are the Holy One of God [You are the Christ, the Son of the living God].
6.70: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered them, “Have I not chosen you?, the Twelve? Yet one of you? is a devil {diabolos}!
6.71: He was speaking about Judas (praise), the son of Simon (hearing) Iscariot. For although Judas was one of the Twelve, he was later to betray Jesus.

7.1: After this, Jesus (YHWH saves) traveled throughout Galilee (circuit). He did not want to travel in Judea (land of praise), because the Jews there were trying to kill Him.
7.2: However, the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles (Sukkot) was near.
7.3: So Jesus’ brothers said to Him, “Leave here and go to Judea (land of praise), so that Your disciples (pupils) there may see the works (acts) You are doing.
7.4: For no one who wants to be known publicly acts in secret. Since You are doing these things, show Yourself to the world.
7.5: For even His own brothers did not believe (have faith) in Him.
7.6: Therefore Jesus (YHWH saves) told them, “Although your? time is always at hand, My time has not yet come.
7.7: The world cannot hate you?, but it hates Me, because I testify that its works (acts) are evil.
7.8: Go up to the feast on your? own. I am not [I am not yet] going up to this feast, because My time has not yet come.
7.9: Having said this, Jesus remained in Galilee (circuit).
7.10: But after His brothers had gone up to the feast, He also went—not publicly, but in secret.
7.11: So the Jews (people of praise) were looking for Him at the feast and asking, “Where is He?
7.12: Many in the crowds were whispering about Him. Some said, “He is a good man. But others replied, “No, He deceives the people.
7.13: Yet no one would speak publicly about Him for fear of the Jews (people of praise).
7.14: About halfway through the feast, Jesus (YHWH saves) went up to the temple courts \the temple/ and began to teach.
7.15: The Jews (people of praise) were amazed and asked, “How did this man attain such learning without having studied?
7.16: “My teaching {didach?} is not My own, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied. “It comes from Him who sent Me.
7.17: If anyone desires to do His will, he will know whether My teaching {didach?s} is from God {Theou} or whether I speak on My own.
7.18: He who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory {doxan}, but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is a man of truth; in Him there is no falsehood (wickedness).
7.19: Has not Moses (drawn out) given you? the law? Yet not one of you? keeps it. Why are you? trying to kill Me?
7.20: “You have a demon, the crowd replied. “Who is trying to kill You?
7.21: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered them, “I did one miracle, and you? are all amazed.
7.22: But because Moses (drawn out) gave you? circumcision, you? circumcise a boy on the Sabbath (not that it is from Moses, but from the patriarchs {pater?n}.)
7.23: If a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses (drawn out) will not be broken, why are you? angry with Me for making the whole man well on the Sabbath?
7.24: Stop judging by outward appearances, and start judging justly.
7.25: Then some of the people of Jerusalem (city of peace) began to say, “Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?
7.26: Yet here (behold) He is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying anything to Him. Have the rulers truly recognized that this is the Christ (the Anointed One)?
7.27: But we know where this man is from. When the Christ (the Anointed One) comes, no one will know where He is from.
7.28: Then Jesus (YHWH saves), still teaching {didask?n} in the temple courts, cried out, “You? know Me, and you? know where I am from. I have not come of My own accord, but He who sent Me is true. You? do not know Him,
7.29: but I know Him, because I am from Him and He sent Me.
7.30: So they tried to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.
7.31: Many in the crowd, however, believed (had faith) in Him and said, “When the Christ (the Anointed One) comes, will He perform more signs than this man?
7.32: When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering these things about Jesus, they and the chief priests sent officers to arrest Him.
7.33: So Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “I am with you? only a little while longer, and then I am going to the One who sent Me.
7.34: You? will look for Me, but you? will not find Me; and where I am, you? cannot come.
7.35: At this, the Jews (people of praise) said to one another, “Where does He intend to go that we will not find Him? Will He go where the Jews are dispersed among the Greeks \Will He go to the Diaspora among the Greeks/, and teach {didaskein} the Greeks?
7.36: What does He mean by saying, ‘You? will look for Me, but you? will not find Me, and, ‘Where I am, you? cannot come
7.37: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus (YHWH saves) stood up and called out in a loud voice, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.
7.38: Whoever believes in Me, as the Scripture has said: ‘Streams of living water will flow from within him.
7.39: He was speaking about the Spirit {Pneumatos}, whom those who believed in Him were later to receive. For the Spirit {Pneuma} had not yet been given [the Holy Spirit was not yet], because Jesus (YHWH saves) had not yet been glorified.
7.40: On hearing these words, some of the people said, “This is truly the Prophet.
7.41: Others declared, “This is the Christ (the Anointed One). But still others asked, “How can the Christ come from Galilee (circuit)?
7.42: Doesn’t the Scripture say that the Christ (the Anointed One) will come from the line of David (beloved) and from Bethlehem (house of bread), the village where David lived ?
7.43: So there was division in the crowd because of Jesus.
7.44: Some of them wanted to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him.
7.45: Then the officers returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why didn’t you? bring Him in?
7.46: “Never has anyone spoken like this man! the officers answered.
7.47: “Have you? also been deceived? replied the Pharisees.
7.48: “Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed (had faith) in Him?
7.49: But this crowd that does not know the law—they are under a curse.
7.50: Nicodemus (victory of the people), who had gone to Jesus earlier and who himself was one of them, asked,
7.51: “Does our law convict a man without first hearing from him to determine what he has done?
7.52: “Aren’t you also from Galilee (circuit)? they replied. “Look into it, and you will see (behold) that no prophet comes out of Galilee.”
7.53: Then each went to his own home.

8.1: But Jesus (YHWH saves) went to the Mount of Olives.
8.2: Early in the morning He went back into the temple courts \the temple/. All the people came to Him, and He sat down to teach them.
8.3: The scribes and Pharisees, however, brought to Him a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before them
8.4: and said, “Teacher {Didaskale}, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.
8.5: In the Law Moses (drawn out) commanded us to stone such a woman. So what do You say?
8.6: They said this to test Him, in order to have a basis for accusing Him. But Jesus (YHWH saves) bent down and began to write on the ground with His finger.
8.7: When they continued to question Him, He straightened up and said to them, “Let him who is without sin among you? be the first to cast a stone at her.
8.8: And again He bent down and wrote on the ground.
8.9: When they heard this [and were convicted by their conscience,], they began to go away one by one, beginning with the older ones, until only Jesus (YHWH saves) was left, with the woman standing there.
8.10: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) straightened up [and saw no one but the woman.] and asked her, “Woman, where are your accusers [where are they]? Has no one condemned you?
8.11: “No one, Lord {kyrie}, she answered. “Then neither do I condemn you, Jesus (YHWH saves) declared. “Now go and sin no more.
8.12: Once again, Jesus (YHWH saves) spoke to the people and said, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will never walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.
8.13: So the Pharisees said to Him, “You are testifying about Yourself; Your testimony is not valid.
8.14: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Even if I testify about Myself, My testimony is valid, because I know where I came from and where I am going. But you? do not know where I came from or where I am going.
8.15: You? judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
8.16: But even if I do judge, My judgment is true, because I am not alone; I am with the Father {pat?r} who sent Me \but it is I and the Father who sent Me/.
8.17: Even in your? own Law it is written that the testimony of two men is valid .
8.18: I am One who testifies about Myself, and the Father {Pat?r}, who sent Me, also testifies about Me.
8.19: “Where is Your Father {Pat?r}? they asked Him. “You? do not know Me or My Father {Patera}, Jesus (YHWH saves) answered. “If you? knew Me, you? would know My Father as well.
8.20: He spoke these words {rh?mata} while teaching {didask?n} in the temple courts, near the treasury. Yet no one seized Him, because His hour had not yet come.
8.21: Again He said to them, “I am going away, and you? will look for Me, but you? will die in your? sin. Where I am going, you? cannot come.
8.22: So the Jews (people of praise) began to ask, “Will He kill Himself, since He says, ‘Where I am going, you? cannot come’?
8.23: Then He told them, “You? are from below; I am from above. You? are of this world; I am not of this world.
8.24: That is why I told you? that you? would die in your? sins. For unless you? believe that I am He, you? will die in your? sins.
8.25: “Who are You? they asked. “Just what I have been telling you? from the beginning, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied.
8.26: “I have much to say about you? and much to judge. But the One who sent Me is truthful, and what I have heard from Him, I tell the world.
8.27: They did not understand that He was telling them about the Father {Patera}.
8.28: So Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “When you? have lifted up the Son of Man, then you? will know that I am He, and that I do nothing on My own, but speak exactly what the Father {Pat?r} has taught Me.
8.29: He who sent Me is with Me. He has not left Me alone, because I always do what pleases Him.
8.30: As Jesus spoke these things, many believed (had faith) in Him.
8.31: So He said to the Jews (people of praise) who had believed Him, “If you? continue in My word, you? are truly My disciples (pupils).
8.32: Then you? will know the truth, and the truth will set you? free.
8.33: “We are Abraham’s descendants (seed), they answered. “We have never been slaves to anyone. How can You say we will be set free?
8.34: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you?, everyone who sins is a slave {doulos} to sin.
8.35: A slave {doulos} does not remain in the house forever \to the age/, but a son remains forever.
8.36: So if the Son sets you? free, you? will be free indeed.
8.37: I know you? are Abraham’s descendants (seed), but you? are trying to kill Me because My word has no place within you?.
8.38: I speak of what I have seen in the presence of the Father, and you? do what you? have heard from your? father.
8.39: “Abraham (father of many) is our father {pat?r}, they replied. “If you? were children of Abraham, said Jesus (YHWH saves), “you? would do the works (acts) of Abraham.
8.40: But now you? are trying to kill Me, a man who has told you? the truth that I heard from God {Theou}. Abraham (father of many) never did such a thing.
8.41: You? are doing the works (acts) of your? father. “We are not illegitimate children, they declared. “Our only Father {Patera} is God Himself.
8.42: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to them, “If God {Theos} were your? Father {Pat?r}, you? would love {?gapate} Me, for I have come here from God {Theou}. I have not come on My own, but He sent Me.
8.43: Why do you? not understand what I am saying? It is because you? are unable to accept My message.
8.44: You? belong to your? father, the devil {diabolou}, and you? want to carry out his desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, refusing to uphold the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, because he is a liar and the father {pat?r} of lies.
8.45: But because I speak the truth, you? do not believe Me!
8.46: Which of you? can prove Me guilty of sin? If I speak the truth, why do you? not believe Me?
8.47: Whoever belongs to God {Theou} hears the words {rh?mata} of God. The reason you? do not hear is that you? do not belong to God.
8.48: The Jews (people of praise) answered Him, “Are we not right to say that You are a Samaritan and You have a demon?
8.49: “I do not have a demon, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “but I honor My Father {Patera}, and you? dishonor Me.
8.50: I do not seek My own glory {doxan}. There is One who seeks it, and He is the Judge.
8.51: Truly, truly, I tell you?, if anyone keeps My word, he will never see death.
8.52: “Now we know that You have a demon! declared the Jews (people of praise). “Abraham (father of many) died, and so did the prophets, yet You say that anyone who keeps Your word will never taste death.
8.53: Are You greater than our father Abraham (father of many)? He died, as did the prophets. Who do You claim to be?
8.54: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “If I glorify Myself, My glory {doxa} means nothing. The One who glorifies Me is My Father {Pat?r}, of whom you? say, ‘He is our [Your] God {Theos}.’
8.55: You? do not know Him, but I know Him. If I said I did not know Him, I would be a liar like you?. But I do know Him, and I keep His word.
8.56: Your? father {pat?r} Abraham (father of many) rejoiced that he would see My day. He saw it and was glad.
8.57: Then the Jews (people of praise) said to Him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and You have seen Abraham (father of many)?
8.58: “Truly, truly, I tell you?, Jesus (YHWH saves) declared, “before Abraham (father of many) was born, I am \before Abraham was, I am!/!
8.59: At this, they picked up stones to throw at Him. But Jesus (YHWH saves) was hidden and went out of the temple area [going through the midst of them, and so He passed by].

9.1: Now as Jesus was passing by, He saw a man blind from birth,
9.2: and His disciples (pupils) asked Him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?
9.3: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but this happened so that the works (acts) of God {Theou} would be displayed in him.
9.4: While it is daytime, we must do [I must do] the works (acts) of Him who sent Me. Night is coming, when no one can work.
9.5: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
9.6: When Jesus had said this, He spit on the ground, made some mud, and applied it to the man’s eyes.
9.7: Then He told him, “Go, wash in the Pool of Siloam (which means “Sent”). So the man went and washed, and came back seeing.
9.8: At this, his neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging began to ask, “Isn’t this the man who used to sit and beg?
9.9: Some claimed that he was, but others said, “No, he just looks like him. But the man kept saying, “I am the one.
9.10: “How then were your eyes opened? they asked.
9.11: He answered, “The man they call Jesus (YHWH saves) made some mud and anointed my eyes, and He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed and received my sight.
9.12: “Where is He? they asked. “I do not know, he answered.
9.13: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
9.14: Now the day on which Jesus (YHWH saves) had made the mud and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.
9.15: So the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. The man answered, “He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and now I can see.
9.16: Because of this, some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God {Theou}, for He does not keep the Sabbath. But others said, “How can a sinful man perform such signs? And there was division among them.
9.17: So once again they asked the man who had been blind, “What do you say about Him, since it was your eyes He opened? “He is a prophet, the man replied.
9.18: The Jews (people of praise) still did not believe (have faith) that the man had been blind and had received his sight until they summoned his parents
9.19: and asked, “Is this your? son, the one you? say was born blind? So how is it that he can now see?
9.20: His parents answered, “We know he is our son, and we know he was born blind.
9.21: But how he can now see or who opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him. He is old enough to speak for himself.
9.22: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews (people of praise). For the Jews had already determined that anyone who confessed Jesus as the Christ (the Anointed One) would be put out of the synagogue.
9.23: That was why his parents said, “He is old enough. Ask him.
9.24: So a second time they called for the man who had been blind and said, “Give glory {doxan} to God {The?}! We know that this man is a sinner.
9.25: He answered, “Whether He is a sinner I do not know. There is one thing I do know: I was blind, but now I see!
9.26: “What did He do to you? they asked. “How did He open your eyes?
9.27: He replied, “I already told you?, and you? did not listen. Why do you? want to hear it again? Do you? also want to become His disciples (pupils)?
9.28: Then they heaped insults on him and said, “You are His disciple (pupil); we are disciples (pupils) of Moses (drawn out).
9.29: We know that God {Theos} spoke to Moses (drawn out), but we do not know where this man is from.
9.30: “That is remarkable indeed! the man said. “You? do not know where He is from, and yet He opened my eyes.
9.31: We know that God {Theos} does not listen to sinners, but He does listen to the one who worships Him and does His will.
9.32: Never before has anyone heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.
9.33: If this man were not from God {Theou}, He could do no such thing.
9.34: They replied, “You were born in utter sin, and you are instructing {didaskeis} us? And they threw him out.
9.35: When Jesus (YHWH saves) heard that they had thrown him out, He found the man and said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man [the Son of God]
9.36: “Who is He, Sir {Kyrie}? he replied. “Tell me so that I may believe in Him.
9.37: “You have already seen Him, Jesus (YHWH saves) answered. “He is the One speaking with you.
9.38: “Lord {Kyrie}, I believe, he said. And he worshiped Jesus.
9.39: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) declared, “For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind may see and those who see may become blind.”
9.40: Some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard this, and they asked Him, “Are we blind too?
9.41: “If you? were blind, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “you? would not be guilty of sin. But since you? claim you can see, your? guilt remains.

10.1: “Truly, truly, I tell you?, whoever does not enter the sheepfold by the gate, but climbs in some other way, is a thief and a robber.
10.2: But the one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
10.3: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen for his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
10.4: When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow (accompany) him because they know his voice.
10.5: But they will never follow (accompany) a stranger; in fact, they will flee from him because they do not recognize his voice.
10.6: Jesus (YHWH saves) spoke to them using this illustration, but they did not understand what He was telling them.
