14.26: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}, whom the Father {Pat?r} will send in My name, will teach {didaxei} you? all things and will remind you? of everything I have told you?.
14.27: Peace I leave with you?; My peace I give to you?. I do not give to you? as the world gives. Do not let your? hearts be troubled; do not be afraid.
14.28: You? heard Me say, ‘I am going away, and I am coming back to you?. If you? loved {?gapate} Me, you? would rejoice that I am going to the Father {Patera}, because the Father {Pat?r} is greater than I.
14.29: And now I have told you? before it happens, so that when it does happen, you? will believe.
14.30: I will not speak with you? much longer, for the prince of this world is coming, and he has no claim on Me.
14.31: But I do exactly what the Father {Pat?r} has commanded Me, so that the world may know that I love {agap?} the Father {Patera}. Get up! Let us go on from here.

15.1: [“] I am the true vine, and My Father {Pat?r} is the keeper of the vineyard.
15.2: He cuts off every branch in Me that bears no fruit, and every branch that does bear fruit, He prunes to make it even more fruitful.
15.3: You? are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you?.
15.4: Remain in Me, and I will remain in you?. Just as no branch can bear fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, neither can you? bear fruit unless you? remain in Me.
15.5: I am the vine and you? are the branches. The one who remains in Me, and I in him, will bear much fruit. For apart from Me you? can do nothing.
15.6: If anyone does not remain in Me, he is like a branch that is thrown away and withers. Such branches are gathered up, thrown into the fire, and burned.
15.7: If you? remain in Me and My words {rh?mata} remain in you?, ask whatever you? wish, and it will be done for you?.
15.8: This is to My Father’s {Pat?r} glory {edoxasth?}, that you? bear much fruit, proving yourselves to be My disciples (pupils).
15.9: As the Father {Pat?r} has loved {?gap?sen} Me, so have I loved {?gap?sa} you?. Remain in My love {agap?}.
15.10: If you? keep My commandments, you? will remain in My love {agap?}, just as I have kept My Father’s commandments and remain in His love.
15.11: I have told you? these things so that My joy may be in you? and your? joy may be complete.
15.12: This is My commandment, that you? love {agapate} one another as I have loved {?gap?sa} you?.
15.13: Greater love {agap?n} has no one than this, that he lay down his life {psych?n} for his friends.
15.14: You? are My friends if you? do what I command you?.
15.15: No longer do I call you? servants {doulous}, for a servant {doulos} does not understand what his master {kyrios} is doing. But I have called you? friends, because everything I have learned from My Father I have made known to you?.
15.16: You? did not choose Me, but I chose you?. And I appointed you? to go and bear fruit—fruit that will remain—so that whatever you? ask the Father {Patera} in My name, He will give you?.
15.17: This is My command to you?: Love {agapate} one another.
15.18: If the world hates you?, understand that it hated Me first.
15.19: If you? were of the world, it would love you as its own. Instead, the world hates you?, because you? are not of the world, but I have chosen you? out of the world.
15.20: Remember the word that I spoke to you?: ‘No servant {doulos} is greater than his master .’ If they persecuted Me, they will persecute you? as well; if they kept My word, they will keep yours? as well.
15.21: But they will treat you? like this because of My name, since they do not know the One who sent Me.
15.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. Now, however, they have no excuse for their sin.
15.23: Whoever hates Me hates My Father {Patera} as well.
15.24: If I had not done among them the works (acts) that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin; but now they have seen and hated both Me and My Father {Patera}.
15.25: But this is to fulfill what is written in their Law: ‘They hated Me without reason .’
15.26: When the Advocate (Comforter) comes, whom I will send to you? from the Father—the Spirit {Pneuma} of truth who proceeds from the Father—He will testify about Me.
15.27: And you? also must testify, because you? have been with Me from the beginning.

16.1: [“] I have told you? these things so that you? will not fall away.
16.2: They will put you? out of the synagogues. In fact, a time is coming when anyone who kills you? will think he is offering a service to God {The?}.
16.3: They will do these things because they have not known the Father {Patera} or Me.
16.4: But I have told you? these things so that when their hour comes, you? will remember that I told you? about them. I did not tell you? these things from the beginning, because I was with you?.
16.5: Now, however, I am going to Him who sent Me; yet none of you? asks Me, ‘Where are You going?
16.6: Instead, your? hearts are filled with sorrow because I have told you? these things.
16.7: But I tell you? the truth, it is for your? benefit that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Advocate (Comforter) will not come to you?; but if I go, I will send Him to you?.
16.8: And when He comes, He will convict the world in regard to sin and righteousness and judgment:
16.9: in regard to sin, because they do not believe in Me;
16.10: in regard to righteousness, because I am going to the Father {Patera} and you? will no longer see Me;
16.11: and in regard to judgment, because the prince of this world has been condemned.
16.12: I still have much to tell you?, but you? cannot yet bear to hear it.
16.13: However, when the Spirit {Pneuma} of truth comes, He will guide you? into all truth. For He will not speak on His own, but He will speak what He hears, and He will declare to you? what is to come.
16.14: He will glorify Me by taking from what is Mine and disclosing it to you?.
16.15: Everything that belongs to the Father {Pat?r} is Mine. That is why I said that the Spirit will take from what is Mine and disclose it to you?.
16.16: In a little while you? will see Me no more, and then after a little while you? will see Me [because I go away to the Father.].
16.17: Then some of His disciples (pupils) asked one another, “Why is He telling us, ‘In a little while you? will not see Me, and then after a little while you? will see Me’ and ‘Because I am going to the Father {Patera}’?
16.18: They kept asking, “Why is He saying, ‘a little while’? We do not understand what He is saying.
16.19: Aware that they wanted to question Him, Jesus said to them, “Are you? asking one another why I said, ‘In a little while you? will not see Me, and then after a little while you? will see Me’?
16.20: Truly, truly, I tell you?, you? will weep and wail while the world rejoices. You? will grieve, but your? grief will turn to joy.
16.21: A woman has pain in childbirth because her time has come; but when she brings forth her child, she forgets her anguish because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
16.22: So also you? have sorrow now, but I will see you? again and your? hearts will rejoice, and no one will take away your? joy.
16.23: In that day you? will no longer ask Me anything. Truly, truly, I tell you?, whatever you? ask the Father {Patera} in My name, He will give you?.
16.24: Until now you? have not asked for anything in My name. Ask and you? will receive, so that your? joy may be complete.
16.25: I have spoken these things to you? in figures of speech. An hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you? this way, but will tell you? plainly about the Father.
16.26: In that day you? will ask in My name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father {Patera} on your? behalf.
16.27: For the Father {Pat?r} Himself loves you?, because you? have loved Me and have believed that I came from God [from the Father].
16.28: I came from the Father and entered the world. In turn, I will leave the world and go to the Father {Patera}.
16.29: His disciples (pupils) said, “See (behold), now You are speaking plainly and without figures of speech.
16.30: Now we understand that You know all things and that You have no need for anyone to question You. Because of this, we believe that You came from God {Theou}.
16.31: “Do you? finally believe? Jesus (YHWH saves) replied.
16.32: “Look (Behold), an hour is coming and has already come when you? will be scattered, each to his own home, and you? will leave Me all alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father {Pat?r} is with Me.
16.33: I have told you? these things so that in Me you? may have peace. In the world you? will have tribulation. But take courage; I have overcome the world!

17.1: When Jesus (YHWH saves) had spoken these things, He lifted up His eyes to heaven and said, “Father {Pater}, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son may glorify You.
17.2: For You granted Him authority over all people \all flesh/, so that He may give eternal life to all those You have given Him.
17.3: Now this is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God {Theon}, and Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One), whom You have sent.
17.4: I have glorified You on earth by accomplishing the work You gave Me to do.
17.5: And now, Father {Pater}, glorify Me in Your presence with the glory {dox?} I had with You before the world existed.
17.6: I have revealed Your name to those You have given Me out of the world. They were Yours; You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.
17.7: Now they know that everything You have given Me comes from You.
17.8: For I have given them the words {rh?mata} You gave Me, and they have received them. They knew with certainty that I came from You, and they believed (had faith) that You sent Me.
17.9: I ask on their behalf. I do not ask on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those You have given Me; for they are Yours.
17.10: All I have is Yours, and all You have is Mine; and in them I have been glorified.
17.11: I will no longer be in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father {Pater}, protect them by Your name, the name You gave Me \Your name, which You gave Me/, so that they may be one as We are one.
17.12: While I was with them, I protected and preserved them by Your name, the name You gave Me. Not one of them has been lost, except the son of destruction (ruin), so that the Scripture would be fulfilled.
17.13: But now I am coming to You; and I am saying these things while I am in the world, so that they may have My joy fulfilled within them.
17.14: I have given them Your word and the world has hated them. For they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
17.15: I am not asking that You take them out of the world, but that You keep them from the evil one (from evil).
17.16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
17.17: Sanctify them by the truth; Your word is truth.
17.18: As You sent Me into the world, I have also sent them into the world.
17.19: For them I sanctify Myself, so that they too may be sanctified by the truth.
17.20: I am not asking on behalf of them alone, but also on behalf of those who will believe in Me through their message,
17.21: that all of them may be one, as You, Father {pater}, are in Me, and I am in You. May they also be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.
17.22: I have given them the glory {doxan} You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one—
17.23: I in them and You in Me—that they may be perfectly united, so that the world may know that You sent Me and have loved {?gap?sas} them just as You have loved Me.
17.24: Father {Pater}, I want those You have given Me to be with Me where I am, that they may see the glory {doxan} You gave Me because You loved {?gap?sas} Me before the foundation of the world.
17.25: Righteous Father {Pater}, although the world has not known You, I know You, and they know that You sent Me.
17.26: And I have made Your name known to them and will continue to make it known, so that the love {agap?} You have for {?gap?sas} Me may be in them, and I in them.

18.1: After Jesus (YHWH saves) had spoken these words, He went out with His disciples (pupils) across the Kidron Valley, where they entered a garden.
18.2: Now Judas (praise) His betrayer also knew the place, because Jesus [Jesus also] had often met there with His disciples (pupils).
18.3: So Judas (praise) brought a band of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees. They arrived at the garden carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
18.4: Jesus (YHWH saves), knowing all that was coming upon Him, stepped forward and asked them, “Whom are you? seeking?
18.5: “Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch), they answered. Jesus said, “I am He. And Judas (praise) His betrayer was standing there with them.
18.6: When Jesus said, “I am He, they drew back and fell to the ground.
18.7: So He asked them again, “Whom are you? seeking? “Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch), they answered.
18.8: “I told you? that I am He, Jesus (YHWH saves) replied. “So if you? are looking for Me, let these men go.
18.9: This was to fulfill the word He had spoken: “I have not lost one of those You have given Me .”
18.10: Then Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) drew his sword and struck the servant {doulon} of the high priest, cutting off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
18.11: “Put your sword back in its sheath! Jesus (YHWH saves) said to Peter (rock). “Shall I not drink the cup the Father {Pat?r} has given Me?
18.12: Then the band of soldiers, with its commander (of a thousand men) and the officers of the Jews (people of praise), arrested Jesus (YHWH saves) and bound Him.
18.13: They brought Him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
18.14: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews (people of praise) that it would be better if one man died for the people.
18.15: Now Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) and another disciple (pupil) were following Jesus (YHWH saves). Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he also went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.
18.16: But Peter (rock) stood outside at the door. Then the disciple (pupil) who was known to the high priest went out and spoke to the doorkeeper, and brought Peter in.
18.17: At this, the servant girl watching the door said to Peter (rock), “Aren’t you also one of this man’s disciples (pupils)? “I am not, he answered.
18.18: Because it was cold, the servants {douloi} and officers were standing around a charcoal fire they had made to keep warm. And Peter (rock) was also standing with them, warming himself.
18.19: Meanwhile, the high priest questioned Jesus (YHWH saves) about His disciples (pupils) and His teaching {didach?s}.
18.20: “I have spoken openly to the world, Jesus (YHWH saves) answered. “I always taught in the synagogues and at the temple, where all the Jews (people of praise) come together. I said nothing in secret.
18.21: Why are you asking Me? Ask those who heard My message. Surely (behold) they know what I said.
18.22: When Jesus had said this, one of the officers standing nearby slapped Him in the face and said, “Is this how You answer the high priest?
18.23: Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “If I said something wrong, testify as to what was wrong. But if I spoke correctly, why did you strike Me?
18.24: Then Annas sent Him, still bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
18.25: Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) was still standing and warming himself. So they asked him, “Aren’t you also one of His disciples (pupils)? He denied it and said, “I am not.
18.26: One of the high priest’s servants {doul?n}, a relative of the man whose ear Peter (rock) had cut off, asked, “Didn’t I see you with Him in the garden?
18.27: Peter (rock) denied it once more, and immediately a rooster crowed.
18.28: Then they led Jesus (YHWH saves) away from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. By now it was early morning, and the Jews did not enter the Praetorium, to avoid being defiled and unable to eat the Passover.
18.29: So Pilate (armed with a spear) went out to them and asked, “What accusation are you? bringing against this man?
18.30: “If He were not a criminal, they replied, “we would not have handed Him over to you.
18.31: “You? take Him and judge Him by your? own law, Pilate (armed with a spear) told them. “We are not permitted to execute anyone, the Jews (people of praise) replied.
18.32: This was to fulfill the word that Jesus (YHWH saves) had spoken to indicate the kind of death He was going to die .
18.33: Pilate (armed with a spear) went back into the Praetorium, summoned Jesus (YHWH saves), and asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews (people of praise)?
18.34: “Are you saying this on your own, Jesus (YHWH saves) asked, “or did others tell you about Me?
18.35: “Am I a Jew? Pilate (armed with a spear) replied. “Your own people {ethnos} and chief priests handed You over to me. What have You done?
18.36: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “My kingdom is not of this world; if it were, My servants would fight to prevent My arrest by the Jews (people of praise). But now My kingdom is not of this realm.
18.37: “Then You are a king! Pilate (armed with a spear) said. “You say that I am a king, Jesus (YHWH saves) answered. “For this reason I was born and have come into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to My voice.
18.38: “What is truth? Pilate (armed with a spear) asked. And having said this, he went out again to the Jews (people of praise) and told them, “I find no basis for a charge against Him.
18.39: But it is your? custom that I release to you? one prisoner at the Passover. So then, do you? want me to release to you? the King of the Jews (people of praise)?
18.40: “Not this man, they shouted, “but Barabbas (Son of Abba)! (Now Barabbas was an insurrectionist.)

19.1: Then Pilate (armed with a spear) took Jesus (YHWH saves) and had Him flogged.
19.2: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns, set it on His head, and dressed Him in a purple robe.
19.3: And they went up to Him again and again, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews (people of praise)! and slapping Him in the face.
19.4: Once again Pilate (armed with a spear) came out and said to the Jews, “Look (behold), I am bringing Him out to you? to let you? know that I find no basis for a charge against Him.
19.5: When Jesus (YHWH saves) came out wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, “Here is the man!
19.6: As soon as the chief priests and officers saw Him, they shouted, “Crucify Him! Crucify Him! “You? take Him and crucify Him, Pilate (armed with a spear) replied, “for I find no basis for a charge against Him.
19.7: “We have a law, answered the Jews (people of praise), “and according to that law He must die, because He declared Himself to be the Son of God {Theou}.
19.8: When Pilate (armed with a spear) heard this statement, he was even more afraid,
19.9: and he went back into the Praetorium. “Where are You from? he asked. But Jesus (YHWH saves) gave no answer.
19.10: So Pilate (armed with a spear) said to Him, “Do You refuse to speak to me? Do You not know that I have authority to release You and authority to crucify You?
19.11: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “You would have no authority over Me if it were not given to you from above. Therefore the one who handed Me over to you is guilty of greater sin.
19.12: From then on, Pilate (armed with a spear) tried to release Him, but the Jews (people of praise) kept shouting, “If you release this man, you are no friend of Caesar (Emporer). Anyone who declares himself a king is defying Caesar.
19.13: When Pilate (armed with a spear) heard these words, he brought Jesus (YHWH saves) out and sat on the judgment seat at a place called the Stone Pavement, which in Hebrew (in Aramaic) is Gabbatha.
19.14: It was the day of Preparation for the Passover, about the sixth hour (about noon). And Pilate said to the Jews (people of praise), “Here is (behold) your? King!
19.15: At this, they shouted, “Away with Him! Away with Him! Crucify Him! “Shall I crucify your? King? Pilate (armed with a spear) asked. “We have no king but Caesar (Emporer), replied the chief priests.
19.16: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified, and the soldiers took Him away.
19.17: Carrying His own cross, He went out to The Place of the Skull, which in Hebrew is called Golgotha.
19.18: There they crucified Him, and with Him two others, one on each side, with Jesus (YHWH saves) in the middle.
19.19: Pilate (armed with a spear) also had a notice posted on the cross. It read: JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS (people of praise).
19.20: Many of the Jews (people of praise) read this sign, because the place where Jesus (YHWH saves) was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.
19.21: So the chief priests of the Jews (people of praise) said to Pilate (armed with a spear), “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews, but only that He said, ‘I am the King of the Jews.
19.22: Pilate (armed with a spear) answered, “What I have written, I have written.
19.23: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus (YHWH saves), they divided His garments into four parts, one for each soldier, with the tunic remaining. It was seamless, woven in one piece from top to bottom.
19.24: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it. Instead, let us cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the Scripture: “They divided My garments among them, and cast lots for My clothing .” So that is what the soldiers did.
19.25: Near the cross of Jesus (YHWH saves) stood His mother and her sister, as well as Mary (beloved) the wife of Clopas and Mary Magdalene (of Magdala).
19.26: When Jesus (YHWH saves) saw His mother and the disciple (pupil) whom He loved {?gapa} standing nearby, He said to His mother, “Woman, here is (behold) your son.
19.27: Then He said to the disciple (pupil), “Here is (behold) your mother. So from that hour, this disciple took her into his home.
19.28: After this, knowing that everything had now been accomplished, and to fulfill the Scripture, Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “I am thirsty .
19.29: A jar of sour wine (A jar of wine vinegar) was sitting there. So they soaked a sponge in the wine, put it on a stalk of hyssop, and lifted it to His mouth .
19.30: When Jesus (YHWH saves) had received the sour wine, He said, “It is finished. And bowing His head, He yielded up His spirit {pneuma}.
19.31: It was the day of Preparation, and the next day was a High Sabbath. In order that the bodies would not remain on the cross during the Sabbath, the Jews (people of praise) asked Pilate (armed with a spear) to have the legs broken and the bodies removed.
19.32: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first man who had been crucified with Jesus, and those of the other.
19.33: But when they came to Jesus (YHWH saves) and saw that He was already dead, they did not break His legs.
19.34: Instead, one of the soldiers pierced His side with a spear, and immediately blood and water flowed out.
19.35: The one who saw it has testified to this, and his testimony is true. He knows that he is telling the truth, so that you? also may believe.
19.36: Now these things happened so that the Scripture would be fulfilled: “Not one of His bones will be broken .”
19.37: And, as another Scripture says: “They will look on the One they have pierced .”
19.38: Afterward, Joseph (he will add) of Arimathea (High place), who was a disciple (pupil) of Jesus (YHWH saves) (but secretly for fear of the Jews (people of praise)), asked Pilate (armed with a spear) to let him remove the body of Jesus. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and removed His body.
19.39: Nicodemus (victory of the people), who had previously come to Jesus at night, also brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about seventy-five pounds <34 kg>.
19.40: So they took the body of Jesus (YHWH saves) and wrapped it in linen cloths with the spices, according to the Jewish burial custom.
19.41: Now there was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
19.42: And because it was the Jewish day of Preparation and the tomb was nearby, they placed Jesus (YHWH saves) there.

20.1: Early on the first day of the week \Now on the first of the Sabbaths, early,/, while it was still dark, Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala) went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the entrance.
20.2: So she came running to Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) and the other disciple (pupil), the one whom Jesus (YHWH saves) loved. “They have taken the Lord {Kyrion} out of the tomb, she said, “and we do not know where they have put Him!
20.3: Then Peter (rock) and the other disciple (pupil) set out for the tomb.
20.4: The two were running together, but the other disciple (pupil) outran Peter (rock) and reached the tomb first.
20.5: He bent down and looked in at the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.
20.6: Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) arrived just after him. He entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
20.7: The cloth {soudarion} that had been around Jesus’ head was rolled up, lying separate from the linen cloths.
20.8: Then the other disciple (pupil), who had reached the tomb first, also went in. And he saw and believed (had faith).
20.9: For they still did not understand from the Scripture that Jesus had to rise from the dead.
20.10: Then the disciples (pupils) returned to their homes.
20.11: But Mary (beloved) stood outside the tomb weeping. And as she wept, she bent down to look into the tomb,
20.12: and she saw two angels (messengers) in white sitting where the body of Jesus (YHWH saves) had lain, one at the head and the other at the feet.
20.13: “Woman, why are you weeping? they asked. “Because they have taken my Lord away {Kyrion}, she said, “and I do not know where they have put Him.
20.14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus (YHWH saves) standing there. But she did not recognize that it was Jesus.
20.15: “Woman, why are you weeping? Jesus (YHWH saves) asked. “Whom are you seeking? Thinking He was the gardener, she said, “Sir, if you have carried Him off, tell me where you have put Him, and I will get Him.
20.16: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to her, “Mary (beloved). She turned and said to Him in Hebrew (in Aramaic), “Rabboni! (which means “Teacher” {Didaskale}).
20.17: “Do not cling to Me, Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “for I have not yet ascended to the Father {Patera}. But go and tell My brothers, ‘I am ascending to My Father and your? Father, to My God {Theon} and your? God.
20.18: Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala) went and announced to the disciples (pupils), “I have seen the Lord {Kyrion}! And she told them what He had said to her.
20.19: It was the first day of the week, and that very evening, while the disciples (pupils) were together with the doors locked for fear of the Jews (people of praise), Jesus (YHWH saves) came and stood among them. “Peace be with you?! He said to them.
20.20: After He had said this, He showed them His hands and His side. The disciples (pupils) rejoiced when they saw the Lord {Kyrion}.
20.21: Again Jesus (YHWH saves) said to them, “Peace be with you?. As the Father {Pat?r} has sent Me, so also I am sending you?.
20.22: When He had said this, He breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}.
20.23: If you? forgive anyone his sins, they are forgiven; if you? withhold forgiveness from anyone, it is withheld.
20.24: Now Thomas (twin) called Didymus (the twin), one of the Twelve, was not with the disciples when Jesus (YHWH saves) came.
20.25: So the other disciples (pupils) told him, “We have seen the Lord {Kyrion}! But he replied, “Unless I see the nail marks in His hands, and put my finger where the nails have been, and put my hand into His side, I will never believe.
20.26: Eight days later, His disciples (pupils) were once again inside with the doors locked, and Thomas (twin) was with them. Jesus (YHWH saves) came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you?.
20.27: Then Jesus said to Thomas (twin), “Put your finger here and look at (behold) My hands. Reach out your hand and put it into My side. Stop doubting and believe.
20.28: Thomas (twin) replied, “My Lord {Kyrios} and my God {Theos}!
20.29: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to him, “Because you have seen Me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.
20.30: Jesus (YHWH saves) performed many other signs in the presence of His disciples (pupils), which are not written in this book (papyrus roll).
20.31: But these are written so that you? may believe (may continue to believe) that Jesus (YHWH saves) is the Christ (the Anointed One), the Son of God {Theou}, and that by believing you? may have life in His name.

21.1: Later, by the Sea of Tiberias (the Sea of Galilee), Jesus (YHWH saves) again revealed (made clear) Himself to the disciples (pupils). He made Himself known in this way:
21.2: Simon (hearing) Peter (rock), Thomas (twin) called Didymus (the twin), Nathanael (God has given) from Cana in Galilee (circuit), the sons of Zebedee (YHWH has given), and two other disciples (pupils) were together.
21.3: Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) told them, “I am going fishing. “We will go with you, they said. So they went out and got into the boat, but caught nothing that night.
21.4: Early in the morning, Jesus (YHWH saves) stood on the shore, but the disciples (pupils) did not recognize that it was Jesus.
21.5: So He called out to them, “Children, do you? have any fish? “No, they answered.
21.6: He told them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you? will find some. So they cast it there, and they were unable to haul it in because of the great number of fish.
21.7: Then the disciple (pupil) whom Jesus (YHWH saves) loved {?gapa} said to Peter (rock), “It is the Lord {Kyrios}! As soon as Simon (hearing) Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on his outer garment (for he had removed it) and jumped into the sea.
21.8: The other disciples (pupils) came ashore in the boat. They dragged in the net full of fish, for they were not far from land, only about a hundred yards <300 ft / 91 m>.
21.9: When they landed, they saw a charcoal fire there with fish on it, and some bread.
21.10: Jesus (YHWH saves) told them, “Bring some of the fish you? have just caught.
21.11: So Simon (hearing) Peter (rock) went aboard and dragged the net ashore. It was full of large fish, 153, but even with so many, the net was not torn.
21.12: “Come, have breakfast, Jesus (YHWH saves) said to them. None of the disciples (pupils) dared to ask Him, “Who are You? They knew it was the Lord {Kyrios}.
21.13: Jesus (YHWH saves) came and took the bread and gave it to them, and He did the same with the fish.
21.14: This was now the third time that Jesus (YHWH saves) appeared to the disciples (pupils) after He was raised from the dead.
21.15: When they had finished eating, Jesus (YHWH saves) asked Simon (hearing) Peter (rock), “Simon son of John (YHWH is gracious), do you love {agapas} Me more than these? “Yes, Lord {Kyrie}, he answered, “You know I love {phil?} You. Jesus replied, “Feed My lambs.
21.16: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon (hearing) son of John (YHWH is gracious), do you love {agapas} Me? “Yes, Lord {Kyrie}, he answered, “You know I love {phil?} You. Jesus told him, “Shepherd My sheep.
21.17: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon (hearing) son of John (YHWH is gracious), do you love Me? Peter (rock) was deeply hurt that Jesus had asked him a third time, “Do you love Me? “Lord {Kyrie}, You know all things, he replied. “You know I love {phil?} You. Jesus (YHWH saves) said to him, “Feed My sheep.
21.18: Truly, truly, I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted; but when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and someone else (others) will dress you and lead you where you do not want to go.
21.19: Jesus said this to indicate the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God {Theon}. And after He had said this, He told him, “Follow (accompany) Me.
21.20: Peter (rock) turned and saw the disciple (pupil) whom Jesus (YHWH saves) loved {?gapa} following them. He was the one who had leaned back against Jesus \reclined on His bosom/ at the supper to ask, “Lord {Kyrie}, who is going to betray You?
21.21: When Peter saw him, he asked, “Lord {Kyrie}, what about him?
21.22: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “If I want him to remain until I return, what is that to you? You follow (accompany) Me!
21.23: Because of this, the rumor spread among the brothers that this disciple (pupil) would not die. However, Jesus (YHWH saves) did not say that he would not die, but only, “If I want him to remain until I return, what is that to you?
21.24: This is the disciple (pupil) who testifies to these things and who has written them down. And we know that his testimony is true.
21.25: There are many more things that Jesus (YHWH saves) did. If all of them were written down, I suppose that not even the world itself would have space for the books (papyrus rolls) that would be written.
